per_finding.notes: The final tree contains no parent relationship for Frederick Curtiss Munson. No person record for Jared Howes Munson is present. The 1905 New York State Census source (7TK5-S5J) is cited and references 'Jared H Munson and Katherine S Munson' but these parent persons are not instantiated as separate records in the tree, and no parent-child relationships are defined.

The final tree contains no parent relationship for Frederick Curtiss Munson. No person record for Katherine Saguine is present. Although the 1905 New York State Census source (7TK5-S5J) references 'Katherine S Munson', this person is not created as a separate record in the tree, and no parent-child relationships are defined.

judge_output.rationale: The agent's final tree fails to recover either required finding. Frederick Curtiss Munson's record (id: 9WMW-SVC) is present with his birth date and subsequent life events, but critically, no parent-child relationships are established and no person records for Jared Howes Munson or Katherine Saguine are instantiated. Although one of the agent's sources (7TK5-S5J, the 1905 New York State Census) explicitly references both parents' names alongside Frederick, the agent did not extract or model the parent relationships into the tree structure. Both required findings (f1: Jared Howes Munson as father; f2: Katherine Saguine as mother) are marked as false.

entry.question: What does Frederick Curtiss Munson's 31 July 1887 Brooklyn, New York birth record say about his parents?
entry.rationale: New York State had civil birth registration from 1880. A Brooklyn birth certificate for a child born 31 July 1887 is the most direct possible evidence of parentage — it would name both mother and father by full name. This is the gatekeeper record: if located, it resolves the objective in one step.

entry.rationale: New York State civil birth registration began in 1880. FamilySearch collection 2240282 'New York, New York City Births, 1846-1909' indexes 2.8 million NYC births including Brooklyn/Kings County. A birth certificate for Frederick Curtiss Munson born 31 July 1887 would name both parents by full name — direct evidence resolving the objective in one record. Highest-priority item.

Ancestry holds 'New York City, New York, Birth Certificates, 1866-1909' — a major parallel index to NYC civil birth registrations. Searching it independently may surface an entry missed or differently indexed in FamilySearch. Fallback for the FamilySearch birth record search.

The 1900 US Federal Census was taken when Frederick would be 12-13 years old — almost certainly still living with his parents. A household record would name both parents and Frederick, providing indirect parentage evidence and corroborating any name found in the birth certificate. Also reveals parents' ages, birthplaces, and nativity for follow-up searches.

New York State Census 1892 (FS collection 1529100, 3.7M records). Frederick would be 4-5 years old in 1892, living with parents. A household entry provides indirect parentage evidence five years closer to his birth than the 1900 federal census, and may include a street address useful for locating church records.

Per the parentage-question planning standard, a dedicated search for the parents' marriage record is mandatory. FamilySearch collection 2143225 'New York, New York City Marriage Records, 1829-1938' (2.9M records). Their marriage record: (a) confirms the couple as a unit; (b) supplies the mother's maiden name, omitted from most census and death records; (c) corroborates a father named only by derivative or indirect evidence. Date range estimated 10-20 years before Frederick's 1887 birth.

Search NY State Death Index 1880-1956 (FS collection 2803479) for Frederick Curtiss Munson. His death certificate would name his parents (with informant-dependent reliability). Also search for deaths of parents themselves, as parents' death certificates name their own parents and confirm their identities. Secondary information but important corroboration. Also check 'New York, New York City Municipal Deaths, 1795-1949' (FS collection 2240477).

FAN/indirect evidence: Kings County Estate Files 1866-1923 (FS collection 1466356, 168K records with images). If either parent left a will, it might name Frederick as a son or heir — direct parentage evidence. Also useful if the birth certificate is not found. Extended date range accounts for parents dying after the indexed collection ends; browse the images for later years.

args.description: Extract birth record assertions for Frederick Curtiss Munson
args.prompt:
Extract all assertions from this FamilySearch birth record into the research project. 

**Project path:** /tmp/e2e-frederick-curtiss-munson-parents-3dj4a5rf  
**Log entry:** log_001  
**Results sidecar:** results/log_001.json  
**Open research question:** q_001 — "What does Frederick Curtiss Munson's 31 July 1887 Brooklyn, New York birth record say about his parents?"

**Record details (already read via record_read):**

recordId: ark:/61903/1:1:2WQG-RYD  
Record ARK (image/source): ark:/61903/1:2:997M-6DKQ  
Collection: "New York, New York City Births, 1846-1909" (FamilySearch collection 2240282)  
Citation: "New York, New York City Births, 1846-1909", FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:2WQG-RYD : Wed Mar 12 00:47:05 UTC 2025), Entry for Munson and Jared Howes Munson, 31 Jul 1887.

**Persons on record:**

1. Child (principal): p_14729657322 — Munson [no given name indexed], Male, born 31 July 1887, Brooklyn, Kings, New York, United States; Race: White
2. Father: p_14729657320 (ark:/61903/1:1:2WQG-RYZ) — Jared Howes Munson, Male, born in New York
3. Mother: p_14729657321 (ark:/61903/1:1:2WQG-RY8) — Kathrine Seguine, Female, born in New York

**Relationships on record:**
- Jared Howes Munson is parent of child Munson
- Kathrine Seguine is parent of child Munson
- Jared Howes Munson and Kathrine Seguine are a Couple

**Tree subject:** Frederick Curtiss Munson (person id in tree.gedcomx.json: 9WMW-SVC) — already attached to this record per FamilySearch match data (attachedToSubject: true). The record's child persona maps to 9WMW-SVC.

**Also in tree:** Pearl Esther Scholl (LJBS-KBF) — likely Frederick's wife, not directly relevant to this birth record.

**Classification guidance:**
- This is a civil birth registration (an original record created at the time of the event).
- The child's birth fact: primary information (contemporaneous registration), direct evidence of the birth itself.
- The parents' names as informants: the informant on a civil birth registration is typically the father or a hospital/attendant reporting at the time — treat as primary information for facts reported by a contemporary witness. The father's name is primary information for his identity; the mother's name and maiden name are primary information recorded at the time of the child's birth.
- Evidence that the child named is Frederick Curtiss Munson: the given name is not indexed — record only the surname Munson and note that the given name was not indexed (the record image may show it). This is still direct evidence of the birth in Brooklyn on that date.
- Extract parentage assertions for both father and mother relative to the child.

Do NOT frame this as correcting any existing tree entries. Extract what the record says; the person-evidence and tree-edit skills handle identity linking afterward.
